title: Lachemilla galioides
intro: Lachemilla galioides, commonly known as creeping rock jasmine, is a low-growing, flowering herb native to the Andes mountain range. The health benefits of Lachemilla galioides include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lachemilla galioides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lachemilla galioides belongs to the family Rosaceae and is classified as a species of flowering plant. It is native to the Andes in South America. The genus Lachemilla is closely related to the genus Potentilla. Lachemilla galioides is often used as an ornamental groundcover due to its low-growing and spreading habit. It is also known as creeping Lachemilla or creeping cinquefoil.
